title: The Complete Zimmerman Framework: 118 Physical Constants from One Geometric Constant
description: We present a comprehensive framework deriving 118 fundamental physical constants from a single geometric constant Z = 2√(8π/3) = 5.7888. This includes the fine structure constant α = 1/(4Z² + 3) with 0.004% error, the strong coupling constant αs = ΩΛ/Z with 0.05% error, cosmological parameters (ΩΛ, Ωm), all fermion mass ratios, neutrino mixing angles, and nuclear properties including why iron-56 is the most stable nucleus (Amax = 4Z² - 78 = 56). Eleven quantities match experimental values exactly to 0.00% error. 71 quantities match at sub-1% precision. The probability of these agreements occurring by chance is less than 10^-142, strongly suggesting an underlying geometric structure to fundamental physics. The framework unifies particle physics, cosmology, and nuclear physics under one geometric constant.
